decisions.decision: SCIR is a dual representation: canonical `SCIR-H` and derivative `SCIR-L`.
decisions.constraint_imposed: `SCIR-L` must not become an independent semantic source of truth.

decisions.decision: First credible product is importer + validator + lowering + reconstruction + benchmark harness.
decisions.constraint_imposed: No mass-market language work in phase 1.

decisions.decision: Every claim is profile-qualified (`R`, `N`, `P`, `D-PY`, `D-JS`).
decisions.constraint_imposed: Unqualified preservation or performance claims are invalid, and monolithic `D` is not a valid canonical profile code.

decisions.decision: Preservation levels are `P0`, `P1`, `P2`, `P3`, `PX`.
decisions.constraint_imposed: No binary â€śpreserved/not preservedâ€ť claims.

decisions.decision: Feature coverage is tiered (`A`, `B`, `C`, `D`).
decisions.constraint_imposed: No vague support language.

decisions.decision: Unsupported and opaque cases must be explicit.
decisions.constraint_imposed: No silent fallback to pseudo-support.

decisions.decision: Benchmark-first evaluation is mandatory.
decisions.constraint_imposed: No AI or performance claim without strong baselines.

decisions.decision: Python subset importer lands before Rust subset importer.
decisions.constraint_imposed: Dynamic-host stress comes before ownership-heavy stress.

decisions.decision: Reconstruction is driven primarily from `SCIR-H`.
decisions.constraint_imposed: `SCIR-L` reconstruction is secondary and non-idiomatic.

decisions.decision: Wasm or equivalent portable execution is the first execution backend path.
decisions.constraint_imposed: Native backend sprawl is deferred.

decisions.decision: Validators and report schemas are part of the trusted core surface.
decisions.constraint_imposed: Specs, schemas, and validators must evolve together.

decisions.decision: Architecture changes require synchronized updates to specs, docs, validator contracts, benchmarks, and this register.
decisions.constraint_imposed: No architecture drift by omission.

decisions.decision: Canonical v0.1 `SCIR-H` includes minimal structured `try/catch`.
decisions.constraint_imposed: Exactly one `catch(x: T)` block is allowed; no `finally`, no multi-catch, and `SCIR-L` lowering remains deferred to Phase 3.

decisions.decision: Canonical v0.1 `SCIR-H` includes minimal channel `select`.
decisions.constraint_imposed: Arms must be explicit channel `send` or `recv`; no default, timeout, fairness, or priority semantics, and `SCIR-L` lowering remains deferred to Phase 3.

decisions.decision: The executable bootstrap `SCIR-H` surface uses compact indentation-sensitive canonical text.
decisions.constraint_imposed: Canonical bootstrap storage uses newline-delimited suites, compact effect rows, direct calls as `f(args)`, explicit mutation via `var` and `set`, intrinsic scalar comparisons, and rejects legacy brace-delimited bootstrap syntax as non-canonical.

decisions.decision: Automated Track `A` gate evaluation follows the published median-ratio doctrine.
decisions.constraint_imposed: `S3` and `K2` use median token ratios; aggregate ratios remain diagnostic only; automated benchmark status fails if an automated kill gate is hit.

decisions.decision: Phase 3 freezes bootstrap `SCIR-L` to the minimal derivative lowering surface already emitted by the executable path.
decisions.constraint_imposed: `SCIR-L` is limited to `const`, `cmp`, `alloc`, `store`, `load`, `call`, `async.resume`, `opaque.call`, and `ret`/`br`/`cond_br` with block parameters as the only merge mechanism until source coverage requires more.

decisions.decision: Phase 4 freezes bootstrap reconstruction as an `SCIR-H`-driven contract with explicit compile/test, provenance, and opaque-accounting checks.
decisions.constraint_imposed: Supported reconstruction is limited to `a_basic_function` and `a_async_await` at `R/P1` and `c_opaque_call` at `D-PY/P3`; `SCIR-L` remains diagnostic-only for reconstruction; rejected Tier `D` cases do not emit reconstruction artifacts.

decisions.decision: Phase 6 is split so Rust safe-subset evidence lands before any optimization claim, and the only new bootstrap semantics are `SCIR-H` field places and `SCIR-L` `field.addr`.
decisions.constraint_imposed: Phase 6A stays fixture-locked to Rust free functions, borrowed-record field places, async, and explicit unsafe boundaries; optimization and Track `D` remain Phase 6B work.

decisions.decision: Phase 6B splits the dynamic-host profile into `D-PY` and `D-JS`, activates executable Track `D` slices for Rust `N` and Python `D-PY`, and keeps benchmark-only `SCIR-L` emitters non-normative.
decisions.constraint_imposed: Monolithic `D` is invalid after migration; `D-JS` remains doctrine-only in Phase 6B; reconstruction stays `SCIR-H`-driven; witness-bearing second-language execution remains deferred.

decisions.decision: Milestone 02 is fixed to the five-case Python bootstrap importer corpus, and broader Python coverage moves to a follow-on expansion plan.
decisions.constraint_imposed: Do not claim Python importer support beyond `a_basic_function`, `a_async_await`, `c_opaque_call`, `d_exec_eval`, and `d_try_except` without a new plan and fixture-backed evidence.

decisions.decision: After Phase 6B, the roadmap prioritizes Python Milestone 02B first, then TypeScript interface-shaped witness evidence as Phase 7, before any broader backend/runtime expansion.
decisions.constraint_imposed: Milestone 02B remains fixture-backed and importer-first unless downstream doctrine widens; `D-JS` stays non-executable in Phase 7 unless a later plan adds explicit validator, preservation, reconstruction, and benchmark gates; no new backend track is introduced by this sequencing.

decisions.decision: The repository maintains a derived autonomous execution queue as an operational handoff surface anchored to the roadmap and active milestone docs.
decisions.constraint_imposed: `EXECUTION_QUEUE.md` may order and decompose work, but it must not override `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`; blocked items must cite exact open questions; importer-only Milestone 02B items and Phase 7 witness items must not widen executable scope by queue prose alone.
